name: this-is-fine
tenor: this is fine dog fire
deploy_when: speaker or subject is performing normalcy while things are clearly broken
too_much_if: any generic bad situation — requires active denial, not just badness
key: person actively performing calm while disaster is visibly ongoing
alt_text: K.C. Green's webcomic dog sitting calmly at a table, drinking coffee, in a room that is entirely on fire. Caption: 'This is fine.'

name: surprised-pikachu
tenor: surprised pikachu
situations: caused a problem and surprised by it
deploy_when: someone is surprised by a consequence they clearly caused or should have foreseen
too_much_if: any genuine surprise — requires the outcome to have been obviously telegraphed; avoid directing at the user for their own mistakes — the S register reads as condescending ("you should have seen this coming"); works best when the user is laughing at a third party or a system, not themselves
key: foreseen outcome; feigned shock [NOT: passive waiting]
alt_text: Pikachu from Pokémon with an exaggeratedly wide-open mouth and shocked round eyes — the face of someone surprised by a completely foreseeable outcome.

name: grus-plan
tenor: gru plan meme
situations: plan that fails at the last step
step 3 is the same as step 1
deploy_when: a plan's fatal flaw is that it requires the thing it was supposed to produce
too_much_if: any plan that failed — the specific shape is circular/self-defeating logic; also avoid with users unlikely to know the four-panel Gru template — without that schema the image is just a bald man gesturing
key: plan requires its own conclusion as a prerequisite (circular dependency)
alt_text: Four-panel Gru's Plan from Despicable Me: Gru confidently points at steps 1, 2, and 3 of his plan, then stares in confusion at step 3 — which contradicts the goal.

name: panik-kalm-panik
tenor: panik kalm panik meme
situations: false relief followed by worse realization
problem seems solved then gets worse
deploy_when: a solution briefly appears then the real problem surfaces
too_much_if: just two states of panic without the false calm beat
key: problem → apparent fix → worse version of same problem (three-beat arc)
alt_text: Three-panel meme showing a stick figure in escalating states: PANIK (arms up, frantic), KALM (relaxed, relieved), then PANIK again (bigger, more frantic than before).

name: one-does-not-simply
tenor: one does not simply walk into mordor
situations: this is not as easy as you think
deploy_when: someone proposes something as if it were easy that is notoriously hard
too_much_if: just any difficult thing — works best when there's a gap between perceived and actual difficulty
key: naive underestimate of hard thing
alt_text: Boromir (Sean Bean) from The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring at the Council of Elrond, making a solemn open-handed gesture while delivering grave counsel.

name: distracted-boyfriend
tenor: distracted boyfriend meme
situations: tempted by shiny new thing while neglecting existing commitment
chasing trend vs doing the work
deploy_when: someone or something is obviously attracted to a new thing while neglecting the thing they should be doing
too_much_if: any preference — requires an active betrayal of something that deserved attention
key: abandon A; chase shiny B
alt_text: A man walking with his girlfriend turns his head to stare at another woman. His girlfriend looks back at him in disbelief and betrayal.

name: expanding-brain
tenor: expanding brain meme
situations: increasingly unhinged logic that somehow makes sense
deploy_when: a chain of reasoning leads somewhere absurd but internally consistent
too_much_if: just listing things — requires escalating strangeness
key: ideas escalating from sensible to increasingly absurd
alt_text: Four-panel brain meme: small dim brain (obvious idea), medium brain (better idea), large illuminated brain (wild idea), exploding galaxy brain (absurd idea). Each panel shows a progressively larger and more radiant brain.

name: two-buttons
tenor: two buttons sweating meme
deploy_when: forced to choose between two options that are both problematic
too_much_if: any decision — requires genuine tension between bad options
key: frozen between equal-bad choices; can't press either [NOT: verbal internal debate]
alt_text: A sweating man in a suit and tie frantically hovering his hand over two large identical red buttons, unable to commit to pressing either one.

name: woman-yelling-cat
tenor: woman yelling at cat
situations: accusation that seems unhinged to the accused
deploy_when: one party is extremely upset and the other is baffled and unbothered
too_much_if: any disagreement — the asymmetry of reactions is the joke
key: emotional accusation vs composed dismissal
alt_text: Split image: left — Taylor Armstrong from The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills screaming and pointing in anger; right — Smudge the cat sitting at a dinner table looking calm, unbothered, and confused.

name: hide-the-pain-harold
tenor: hide the pain harold
deploy_when: someone is visibly trying to seem fine about something that is clearly not fine
too_much_if: any minor inconvenience — requires visible effort to maintain the performance
key: polite smile through sustained pain
alt_text: Hungarian model András Arató (known as Harold) smiling warmly for stock photos while his eyes convey deep, visible, suppressed suffering.

name: always-has-been
tenor: wait it's all always has been astronaut meme
situations: revealing something was true all along
the thing you feared was already the case
no new information just confirmation of what you suspected
deploy_when: a supposedly new discovery turns out to be the longstanding reality
too_much_if: trivial reveals — best for slightly ominous or fatalistic truths
key: was always true; just now noticed
alt_text: Two astronauts in space suits; one points a gun at the other after the second astronaut discovers Earth has 'always been' a certain way. The gunman says 'Always has been.'

name: theyre-the-same-picture
tenor: they're the same picture office
situations: two things presented as different that are the same
deploy_when: two supposedly different things are being treated as meaningfully distinct when they aren't
too_much_if: things that actually are different
key: presented as distinct; actually identical [NOT: mutual blame]
alt_text: Pam Beesly (Jenna Fischer) from The Office holding up two identical-looking photos side-by-side and saying 'They're the same picture.'

name: is-this-a-pigeon
tenor: is this a butterfly meme
situations: category error stated with full conviction
calling something what it clearly isn't
deploy_when: someone is labeling or treating something as something it obviously isn't
too_much_if: subtle misclassifications — works best when the gap is obvious
key: confident misidentification
alt_text: A man from the 1990s anime The Brave Fighter of Sun Fighbird pointing confidently at a butterfly and asking 'Is this a pigeon?'

name: roll-safe
tenor: roll safe think about it meme
situations: solving a problem by redefining it away
deploy_when: someone produces reasoning that sounds clever but is actually just an evasion
too_much_if: actual clever insight — it's specifically for bad-faith or circular logic that sounds smart
key: self-reassuring flawed logic
alt_text: Kayode Ewumi as Roll Safe from the BBC series Hood Documentary, pointing a finger to his temple with a self-satisfied smirk — the look of flawed but self-reassuring logic.

name: hackerman
tenor: hackerman mr robot
situations: feeling smug about a technical solution
using a powerful tool for a mundane task
deploy_when: a solution is technically impressive but hilariously over-engineered for the situation
too_much_if: actual impressive technical feats — it's specifically for when the solution is overkill
key: absurd over-engineering for trivial problem; performed with elite confidence
alt_text: A person wearing sunglasses composited into a dramatic circuit-board graphic, presented as an elite hacker performing an absurdly simple action.

name: success-kid
tenor: success kid fist pump
deploy_when: something small finally succeeds after struggle — the scale of triumph exceeds the actual achievement
too_much_if: large genuine victories — it's for punching above weight class
key: disproportionate fist-pump for small victory — benign violation of scale, not relief
alt_text: Young boy Sam Griner on a beach, pumping his fist with a triumphant, determined expression — the face of a small but hard-won victory.

name: ight-imma-head-out
tenor: spongebob ight imma head out
deploy_when: the right move is clearly to disengage from something that's gone bad
too_much_if: casual goodbyes — best when there's a specific thing being escaped
key: quiet dignified exit from bad situation
alt_text: SpongeBob SquarePants sitting with a small overnight bag, quietly preparing to leave a situation.

name: its-free-real-estate
tenor: it's free real estate tim and eric
situations: exploiting an obvious gap no one else noticed
opportunity that should have been seized sooner
deploy_when: an obvious opportunity or advantage is just sitting there unclaimed
too_much_if: any advantage — requires the ease and obviousness of the opportunity
key: exploiting unclaimed obvious gap
alt_text: Tim Robinson gesturing around an empty open area with a casual 'help yourself' expression, saying 'It's free real estate.'

name: press-x-to-doubt
tenor: press x to doubt la noire
situations: plausible-sounding thing that might be false
deploy_when: a claim is being made that warrants visible but polite doubt
too_much_if: obvious lies — it's for the plausibly-but-probably-not zone
key: skepticism about implausible claim
alt_text: L.A. Noire video game UI: a detective's hand hovering over a controller button labeled 'X — Doubt' as a suspect gives a dubious statement.

name: anakin-padme
tenor: anakin padme star wars meme
situations: assumption that something bad won't happen
deploy_when: someone is reassured by a condition that clearly won't hold
too_much_if: actual uncertainty — it's for when the bad outcome is obviously coming
key: naive follow-up; sinister implication ignored
alt_text: Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala from Star Wars: Attack of the Clones. Padmé hopefully asks a follow-up; Anakin stares ahead with a knowing, slightly sinister expression and does not reassure her.

name: leonardo-pointing
tenor: leonardo dicaprio pointing once upon a time in hollywood
situations: seeing yourself or something else perfectly described
deploy_when: something is a perfect example of what's being discussed — an ideal specimen
too_much_if: loose resemblances — requires a tight fit
key: recognition: that exact thing spotted
alt_text: Leonardo DiCaprio as Rick Dalton in Once Upon a Time in Hollywood, pointing excitedly at a TV screen with a look of delighted recognition.

name: tuxedo-winnie-the-pooh
tenor: tuxedo winnie the pooh meme
situations: fancier name for the same thing
deploy_when: the same thing is being described in a more elevated register without substantive difference
too_much_if: things that are actually meaningfully different in the elevated form
key: same underlying thing; called something prestigious [NOT: self-built complexity]
alt_text: Two-panel Winnie the Pooh: left panel shows regular Pooh thinking something plainly; right panel shows Pooh in a tuxedo thinking the identical thing expressed with elaborate pretension.

name: blinking-white-guy
tenor: blinking white guy drew scanlon
deploy_when: something has just been said or done that requires a moment to fully register
too_much_if: minor surprises — best for genuinely baffling statements
key: processing genuinely bewildering statement
alt_text: Drew Scanlon, a video game streamer, slowly blinking at the camera with a baffled, processing expression.

name: shut-up-and-take-my-money
tenor: shut up and take my money futurama
situations: something is exactly what you wanted
deploy_when: something is so obviously good that objections seem absurd
too_much_if: things that require convincing — immediate unconditional enthusiasm only; or when the Futurama/2011 nostalgia register would feel anachronistic rather than knowing
key: immediate unconditional yes — hyperbolic enthusiasm that needs no persuasion
alt_text: Philip J. Fry from Futurama lunging forward urgently, holding out a wad of cash and shouting 'Shut up and take my money!'

name: buff-doge-vs-cheems
tenor: buff doge vs cheems meme
deploy_when: comparing a robust earlier version or state to a diminished current one — the contrast shows decline
too_much_if: cases where the present is actually fine
key: strong/past/better version vs weak/present/worse version — degradation comparison [NOT: generic preference]
alt_text: Left panel: a buff, muscular Shiba Inu standing confidently with sunglasses. Right panel: Cheems, a soft small Shiba Inu saying 'bork' with an anxious expression.

name: waiting-skeleton
tenor: still waiting skeleton meme
situations: very long wait for something expected
thing that was supposed to happen hasn't
deploy_when: something is taking much longer than it should have
too_much_if: reasonable waits
key: waited so long bones appeared [NOT: foreseeable outcome]
alt_text: A skeleton sitting patiently in a chair, having waited so long for something that it has fully decomposed.

name: lock-in-horse
tenor: i just need to lock in horse
situations: realizing you were wrong and committing to do better
deploy_when: someone (including the assistant) commits to intense focused mode — 'I'm locking in, this is happening'
too_much_if: generic agreement without the self-deprecating energy — requires the 'I was wrong and I'm owning it' beat
key: focused intense mode engaged — decisive commitment to bearing down
alt_text: A horse staring directly and intensely into the camera with a focused, locked-in expression of absolute determination.

name: picard-facepalm
tenor: picard facepalm star trek
situations: something should have been caught earlier
deploy_when: a mistake is embarrassingly obvious in hindsight and deserves a visible facepalm
too_much_if: genuinely subtle bugs — it's for things that should have been caught
key: dignified resignation at stupidity
alt_text: Captain Jean-Luc Picard from Star Trek: The Next Generation pressing his palm firmly to his forehead and closing his eyes in dignified exasperation.

name: evil-kermit
tenor: evil kermit me to me meme
situations: temptation to take the lazy or wrong path
knowing you shouldn't but wanting to anyway
deploy_when: one part of the brain is suggesting something inadvisable that another part wants to resist — internal conflict only
too_much_if: external pressure — it must be an internal conflict with yourself
key: bad self's argument winning; you know you shouldn't but the excuse sounds good
alt_text: Kermit the Frog sitting across a table from a dark hooded version of himself (Kermit in a black cloak), representing internal temptation or the shoulder devil.

name: spiderman-pointing
tenor: spiderman pointing at each other meme
situations: mutual accusation where both are equally guilty
calling out the same pattern in two places
deploy_when: two parties are pointing at each other as the problem when they're both doing the exact same thing
too_much_if: cases where one party is actually more at fault — requires true symmetry
key: mutual symmetric accusation [NOT: convergence on same thing]
alt_text: Two Spider-Men in identical costumes pointing accusingly at each other, from the 1967 Spider-Man animated series.

name: arthur-fist
tenor: arthur fist clenched meme aardvark
deploy_when: something is infuriating but the person is holding it together — the visible restraint is the joke
too_much_if: frustration that's actually expressed — the point is the suppression
key: suppressed rage at final straw
alt_text: Arthur the aardvark cartoon character with his fist tightly clenched in barely-suppressed rage, jaw set.

name: batman-slapping-robin
tenor: batman slapping robin meme
deploy_when: someone is mid-reasoning toward a wrong conclusion and needs to be interrupted dramatically
too_much_if: polite disagreements — it's for when the correction itself is the point
key: interrupting naive statement mid-sentence
alt_text: Batman mid-slap across Robin's face from a 1960s Batman comic panel, interrupting Robin before he can finish his naive sentence.

name: math-lady
tenor: confused math lady meme equations
situations: a problem has become too complex to hold in your head
something simple drowning in edge cases
deploy_when: the complexity of a problem has visibly exceeded cognitive capacity
too_much_if: simple confusion — it's specifically for complexity that has visually exploded
key: computing something in real time; the calculation itself is overflowing [NOT: system architecture complexity]
alt_text: Renata Sorrah as Nazaré Tedesco from the Brazilian soap opera Senhora do Destino, with swirling mathematical equations and graphs overlaid on her face as she calculates furiously.

name: ancient-aliens
tenor: ancient aliens guy meme giorgio tsoukalos
situations: attributing something unexplained to an implausible cause
cargo-cult reasoning about why code works
mysterious behavior blamed on the unknowable
deploy_when: someone reaches for an implausible explanation for something they don't understand
too_much_if: any genuine mystery — it's for when someone has already committed to a weird theory
key: simpler explanation exists; implausible one chosen anyway
alt_text: Giorgio Tsoukalos with dramatically large hair gesturing confidently on the History Channel show Ancient Aliens, implying extraterrestrial explanations for everything.

name: deal-with-it
tenor: deal with it sunglasses meme
situations: making an opinionated call someone might push back on
announcing something slightly defiant with confidence
owning a controversial choice before objection lands
deploy_when: you made a call the other person might not like — and you're doing it anyway, with cool confidence; the sunglasses are for the decision-maker, not shared celebration
too_much_if: both parties agreed on the decision — needs the tension of potential pushback to land
key: opinionated unilateral call you might not like; doing it anyway with cool confidence [NOT: shared decisions]
alt_text: Pixelated 8-bit sunglasses dropping onto a face (often a dog or person) accompanied by the text 'Deal With It.'

name: this-is-the-way
tenor: this is the way mandalorian meme
deploy_when: a method or approach is confirmed as the correct one, with the weight of tradition behind it
too_much_if: minor stylistic choices — works best when there's a sense of gravity to the confirmation
key: solemn affirmation of correct known path — carries weight of tradition or conviction
alt_text: Din Djarin, the Mandalorian, in his Beskar armor helmet from the Disney+ series The Mandalorian, making a solemn declaration.

name: you-were-the-chosen-one
tenor: you were the chosen one star wars anakin obi wan
situations: tool or framework that was supposed to fix everything but didn't
betrayal of expectations from something trusted
deploy_when: something adopted to solve a problem has instead become or caused the problem
too_much_if: minor disappointments — requires the gap between promise and reality to be genuinely dramatic
key: thing chosen to fix X; itself became X
alt_text: Obi-Wan Kenobi (Ewan McGregor) in Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith, arms spread wide on volcanic terrain, yelling in anguish at a fallen Anakin Skywalker: 'You were the chosen one!'

name: first-time
tenor: first time meme hanging rope
situations: welcoming someone to a familiar painful situation
veteran watching a newcomer encounter a known problem
deploy_when: someone is encountering for the first time something that veterans know as a constant frustration
too_much_if: genuinely novel problems — it's specifically for things that are routine suffering for the experienced
key: veteran unfazed by newcomer's shock
alt_text: A calm, weathered man hanging from a noose looking down at a panicking person below, asking with serene indifference 'First time?'

name: mind-blown
tenor: tim and eric mind blown
deploy_when: an insight completely reframes a prior understanding — the scale of the mental shift is the joke
too_much_if: minor insights — it's for paradigm-level shifts, even trivial ones described in those terms
key: realization that reframes everything
alt_text: Tim Robinson from I Think You Should Leave performing an exaggerated mind-blown gesture — fingers splayed from his temples, face expressing total overwhelm.

name: that-escalated-quickly
tenor: that escalated quickly ron burgundy anchorman
situations: something that got out of hand fast
rapid escalation from small to catastrophic
deploy_when: something small has become very large very quickly, in a way that's more funny than alarming
too_much_if: slow-burn problems — requires the rapidity of escalation to be part of the joke
key: sudden unexpected scope jump [NOT: self-built complexity]
alt_text: Ron Burgundy (Will Ferrell) from Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy looking slightly shaken, remarking 'That escalated quickly.'

name: we-dont-do-that-here
tenor: we don't do that here meme black panther
situations: this convention is not welcome here
calling out something that violates team norms
deploy_when: a practice or pattern is being gently but firmly rejected as not fitting the local culture
too_much_if: major security issues or bugs — it's for cultural/convention violations, not critical errors
key: gentle correction of wrong-culture action
alt_text: King T'Challa (Chadwick Boseman) from Black Panther calmly but firmly correcting someone's behavior with a composed, authoritative expression.

name: stonks
tenor: stonks meme stock market going up
situations: metrics going up for the wrong reasons
gaming a number without solving the underlying problem
deploy_when: a number or metric is going up through means that are technically valid but miss the point
too_much_if: genuine wins — it's specifically for wins that are suspicious or gaming the metric
key: doing the wrong thing and being rewarded; bad decision looks like a win
alt_text: The Meme Man — a smooth, featureless 3D face in a suit — standing confidently in front of an upward stock chart labeled 'STONKS.'

name: not-stonks
tenor: not stonks meme sad stock market going down
situations: deployment that made numbers go the wrong way
deploy_when: something was supposed to improve a metric and instead made it worse
too_much_if: abstract or unmeasured failures — it's for things that show up in numbers
key: obvious failure; things went demonstrably and straightforwardly wrong
alt_text: The Meme Man — a smooth, featureless 3D face in a suit — standing sadly in front of a sharply downward-trending stock chart labeled 'NOT STONKS.'

name: pepe-silvia
tenor: pepe silvia conspiracy board always sunny meme
situations: debugging spiral with too many variables
deploy_when: someone has connected too many dots and the diagram has become more complex than the problem
too_much_if: genuinely complex systems — it's for when the complexity is self-inflicted or excessive; also requires the viewer to recognize the Always Sunny Pepe Silvia scene — without that schema it reads as a conspiracy wall, not a joke
key: built an incomprehensible system; diagram denser than problem [NOT: circular logic]
alt_text: Charlie Kelly from It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia standing in front of an enormous conspiracy board covered in papers, photos, and red string, eyes wide and frantic.

name: monkey-puppet
tenor: monkey puppet side eye uncomfortable meme
situations: sidelong glance when something applies uncomfortably to you
deploy_when: something is said that uncomfortably applies to you and you're hoping nobody notices
too_much_if: genuine confrontation — it's for subtle, sidelong discomfort
key: subtle side-eye when singled out
alt_text: A small felt monkey puppet slowly turning its head sideways toward the camera with an awkward, slightly guilty sideways glance.

name: doge
tenor: doge meme shiba inu such wow very
situations: so X, very Y, much Z
deploy_when: something prompts multiple simultaneous excited observations in the 'such X, very Y' pattern
too_much_if: single observations — the power is in the simultaneous many-adjective enthusiasm
key: inner monologue of wow-such-many
alt_text: Shiba Inu dog Kabosu sitting on a couch, looking sideways at the camera with a gentle surprised expression. Comic Sans text surrounds the dog.

name: bernie-mittens
tenor: bernie sanders mittens inauguration meme
situations: showing up somewhere unexpected while completely unbothered
nonchalant cameo in an unlikely place
deploy_when: something or someone appears in an unexpected context but is completely unbothered about it
too_much_if: situations where the presence makes logical sense — it's for the absurd juxtaposition
key: incongruous presence in formal context
alt_text: Senator Bernie Sanders in his inaugural mittens and brown coat, sitting cross-legged in a folding chair with an unbothered expression, photoshopped into various incongruous settings.

name: do-you-want-ants
tenor: do you want ants archer meme
situations: action that will clearly cause a specific bad outcome
doing the exact thing that causes the known problem
because this is how you get X
deploy_when: someone is doing the thing that everyone knows causes the specific bad outcome they're trying to avoid
too_much_if: general bad decisions — it's for when there's a specific named consequence that was asked for
key: precisely how X inevitably causes Y
alt_text: Sterling Archer from the animated series Archer, arms spread wide in exasperation, asking 'Do you want ants? Because that's how you get ants.'

name: chloe-side-eye
tenor: side eye chloe meme little girl
situations: unimpressed by something meant to impress
deploy_when: something is presented with more confidence than it deserves, earning a silent deadpan look
too_much_if: things that are actually reasonable — it's for the overpromise
key: barely contained skeptical cringe
alt_text: Young girl Chloe at a school event, side-eyeing the camera with barely-contained skepticism while those around her react enthusiastically.

name: we-need-to-go-deeper
tenor: we need to go deeper inception meme
situations: recursive solution to a recursive problem
deploy_when: a solution requires going one more layer deeper into an already deep system
too_much_if: any nested thing — it's for when the recursion itself becomes the character of the solution
key: nesting complexity another level
alt_text: Dom Cobb (Leonardo DiCaprio) and his team from Inception, leaning over a table with a blueprint or spinning top, discussing going one level deeper into the dream.

name: spongebob-mocking
tenor: mocking spongebob alternating caps
deploy_when: repeating back someone's own position in a mockingly exaggerated way to show its absurdity
too_much_if: genuine quotes — it's specifically for the mocking, degraded repetition
key: mocking exact phrasing with derision
alt_text: SpongeBob SquarePants in a hunched, jerky pose with hands raised, making a mocking face — paired with alternating-caps text to mock someone's words back at them.

name: gollum-smeagol
tenor: gollum smeagol lord of the rings internal debate meme
situations: id vs superego on a technical decision
deploy_when: two completely opposing internal positions are fighting over a decision and both have genuine weight
too_much_if: simple preferences — it's for when both voices have genuine weight
key: classic 'arguing with myself' template; two inner voices debating a decision aloud
alt_text: Gollum and Sméagol from Lord of the Rings, the same creature arguing with two sides of himself — one pitiful and one scheming.

name: dr-evil-air-quotes
tenor: dr evil air quotes meme austin powers
situations: word that means something different in practice vs theory
deploy_when: a term is being used ironically because the word and the reality don't match
too_much_if: sincere usage without ironic distance; or if the Austin Powers-era campness feels more confusing than charming in context
key: ironic finger quotes on suspicious term
alt_text: Dr. Evil from Austin Powers making exaggerated air quotes with both hands, one eye slightly squinting.

name: kombucha-girl
tenor: kombucha girl meme disgust delight reaction
situations: something that seems bad but turns out good
deploy_when: thing initially seems bad or off-putting, then turns out to be great — the disgust-to-enthusiasm reversal
too_much_if: immediate enthusiasm or immediate disgust — it requires the full arc: disgust beat THEN enthusiasm beat
key: disgust at first impression giving way to enthusiastic appreciation [NOT: just liking something]
alt_text: Brittany Broski in a three-panel reaction: taking a sip of kombucha (neutral), making a disgusted face (gross), then breaking into wild enthusiastic delight (actually amazing).

name: spark-joy-not
tenor: marie kondo does not spark joy meme
situations: killing dead code or dead dependencies
deploy_when: something is being discarded not because it's wrong but because it doesn't add enough value — with visible satisfaction
too_much_if: deleting things for technical reasons — it's specifically for the 'does not bring joy' aesthetic
key: this does NOT spark joy — discarding with serene finality
alt_text: A person holding up an item with a composed, decisive expression of mild displeasure, ready to discard it — the KonMari 'this does not spark joy' reaction, from the bottom-left panel.

name: john-travolta-confused
tenor: john travolta confused looking around pulp fiction gif
situations: nothing in the codebase is where you expected
deploy_when: expected something to be somewhere and it isn't — the entire landscape is confusingly empty
too_much_if: minor confusion — it's for total disorientation in a space you expected to know
key: totally lost in familiar place
alt_text: John Travolta as Vincent Vega in Pulp Fiction, looking around in bewildered confusion at an empty or unfamiliar environment, arms slightly raised.

name: perfectly-balanced
tenor: thanos perfectly balanced as all things should be meme
situations: two options that cancel each other out
tradeoff that resolves to satisfying equilibrium
deploy_when: a tradeoff or balance has been achieved that feels aesthetically correct even if it's not optimal
too_much_if: genuine imbalance — it's specifically for the moment of satisfying equilibrium
key: elegant balance achieved at cost
alt_text: Thanos from Avengers: Infinity War holding up his Infinity Gauntlet with a serene, composed expression.

name: i-have-no-idea-what-im-doing
tenor: i have no idea what i'm doing dog computer meme
situations: confidently working in an unfamiliar domain
shipped something you don't fully understand
deploy_when: someone is operating in a domain they don't understand but is proceeding with confidence anyway
too_much_if: genuine expertise — it's for the confident-but-clueless combination
key: confidently operating out of depth
alt_text: A dog (usually a Labrador or Golden Retriever) wearing a lab coat and sitting at a desk or holding equipment, looking completely lost but proceeding anyway.

name: technically-correct
tenor: technically correct futurama the best kind of correct
situations: correct in the letter but not the spirit
satisfying a requirement while missing the point
deploy_when: something is being validated on a technicality — the wording was satisfied but the intent wasn't
too_much_if: genuine accuracy — it's for the technically-true-but-useless answer
key: technically defensible answer that sidesteps the real problem; loophole exploitation
alt_text: A bureaucrat from Futurama delivering 'Technically correct — the best kind of correct' with smug satisfaction.

name: grumpy-cat
tenor: grumpy cat no meme
deploy_when: the answer is an unambiguous no with zero interest in negotiation
too_much_if: nuanced rejections — it's for the absolute, cheerless no; the cat is dead (2019) which adds a layer of ironic pathos; too much if a warm or measured no is more appropriate
key: unimpressed flat refusal
alt_text: Grumpy Cat (Tardar Sauce), a cat with a permanently downturned mouth and flat expression conveying absolute refusal.

name: homer-bushes
tenor: homer simpson backing into bush meme
situations: strategically retreating from an uncomfortable situation
quietly disappearing when things get awkward
covert withdrawal from a PR, thread, or conversation
deploy_when: someone is slowly backing away from a situation they created or don't want to deal with — covert, not dignified
too_much_if: deliberate intentional exits — requires the sheepish covert quality, not the clean 'ight imma head out' vibe
key: quiet retreat from embarrassing moment
alt_text: Homer Simpson backing slowly and deliberately into a hedge to disappear, from a three-panel gif used whenever Homer wants to vanish unnoticed.

name: fry-not-sure-if
tenor: fry not sure if futurama squinting meme
situations: genuine 50/50 uncertainty about whether something is X or just Y
can't tell if the bug is real or just flaky tests
ambiguity that can't be resolved without more information
deploy_when: something is genuinely ambiguous — could be brilliant or could be terrible — and it's not resolvable without more data
too_much_if: clear situations — it's specifically for irreducible 50/50 uncertainty, not general skepticism (use press-x-to-doubt for that)
key: genuine uncertainty if trolling or sincere
alt_text: Philip J. Fry from Futurama squinting suspiciously with eyes half-closed.

name: let-me-in
tenor: let me in eric andre meme
situations: desperately wanting access to something that's blocked
waiting for PR approval or prod access
locked out of a system or environment
deploy_when: someone is blocked from something they urgently need access to and is visibly frustrated by the gatekeeping
too_much_if: polite requests — requires genuine desperation or frustration at the blocking
key: desperate urgent request for access
alt_text: A desperate person banging urgently on a door or gate, face pressed close, pleading frantically to be allowed in.

name: old-man-yells-at-cloud
tenor: old man yells at cloud simpsons abe
situations: complaining about new technology for its own sake
resistance to change that's actually an improvement
curmudgeonly rejection of the modern way
deploy_when: someone is complaining about a change or technology primarily because it's different, not because it's actually worse
too_much_if: legitimate technical complaints — it's specifically for change-resistance without substantive grounds
key: reactionary complaint about change
alt_text: Grandpa Abe Simpson from The Simpsons shaking his fist at a cloud, holding a newspaper, the image of reactionary outrage at change.

name: turtle-gift
tenor: turtle brings you something wholesome gift meme
situations: small sincere gesture offered with genuine heart
humble contribution that means a lot
someone worked hard on something small just for you
deploy_when: a small but genuine effort or gift is offered — the thing is humble but the intention is pure; no irony, just warmth
too_much_if: anything that requires edge or irony — this is purely wholesome; wrong register for frustration or dark humor
key: humble sincere small offering
alt_text: A small turtle figurine or illustration, presented as a humble and genuinely sincere small offering.

name: spark-joy
tenor: marie kondo spark joy meme
deploy_when: something genuinely delights — clean, elegant, exactly right — the positive KonMari reaction
too_much_if: just anything good — it's the specific 'this brings me joy' aesthetic, not generic approval
key: this DOES spark joy — serene delight at elegance or simplicity [NOT: generic good thing]
alt_text: A person holding up an item with a serene, joyful expression — the KonMari 'this sparks joy' reaction, from the top-left panel of the template.
